programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple in West Sussex to find their first shared home together on a budget of £700,000, and meets a craftsman keeping the art of trug making alive. The presenter also finds time to meet master craftsmen keeping the county's traditional trug-making heritage alive(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Andy and Danielle have moved into a new home in Teignmouth, Devon, and are desperate for a garden that reminds them of their wonderful holidays in Morocco. Luckily for them, Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich hope to make their dream a reality and design a Marrakesh rhiad-inspired back garden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Caner Rachael South restores a wicker cradle that has been in use for generations. With a new addition to the family on the way the owner hopes to be able to pass this on to her grandchild and keep the heirloom alive. Meanwhile, Brenton West fixes a much-loved powder compact, made in the shape of a piano. Toy restorers Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch add the finishing touch with a new, hand-sewn pink powder pouf and a wall clock is also rejuvenated(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: Born in 1962 to Nigerian parents studying medicine in London, Kemi Nevins now runs a successful cafe in Abercynon serving up delicious food. However, none of that she offers reflects her Nigerian background. Now Kemi's children are adults themselves, they would like to know more about their heritage, so the Welsh-Italian cook visits Kemi to help her reconnect to her roots through her love of food(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat tries to charm Kheerat in the hope of keeping her home - and while he agrees, he increases their rent at the same time, which does not go down well with Mo and Jean. Sharon and Louise assume that Phil's bad mood is down to baby Peggy still being in hospital. Jay leaves Stuart in charge once more only to regret when he and Rainie cover the place in Christmas decorations(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Charlie Brooker assumes the role of host, with journalist and broadcaster Emma Barnett and comedian Phil Wang joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Sue Johnston, Alice Levine and Bob Mortimer join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Taking a place on Graham's festive sofa, from the new blockbuster Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker are John Boyega and Daisy Ridley, alongside Superman himself, Henry Cavill, who is appearing in new Netflix fantasy drama series The Witcher. Ruth Jones and Rob Brydon, reunited for the much-anticipated Gavin & Stacey Christmas special, also appear. Plus, Robbie Williams performs a song from his new album The Christmas Present, and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the influence of Wales' different performance traditions on the nation's comedy and on its biggest stars. He discovers the lasting legacy of the popular 70s double act Ryan and Ronnie on some of today's biggest performers, finds out how the folk club scene helped produce Max Boyce and sees how Rhod Gilbert has inspired a new generation of performers - including the latest winner of the Edinburgh comedy award Jordan Brookes(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The Priest takes Fleabag for some quiet reflection, leading to an unexpected revelation. After a day spent looking back on painful memories, she turns to him for solace, only to find more trouble. Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, with Andrew Scott(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The two finalists take on their last challenge to win an investment from Lord Sugar, with contestants from the series returning to aid them in their endeavours. In an intensive three-day challenge, the finalists must create a new brand for their company, produce a digital billboard and make a television advert, before pitching to Lord Sugar and a room full of industry experts at London's City Hall. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is crowned the winner(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: Tom Allen meets this year's winner and runner-up, as well as Lord Sugar, to look back over the past 12 weeks of the business-based reality show. Featuring contributions from former contestants and special guests(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Tina Daheley present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jane Baxter and Nathan Outlaw, plus special guest Frank Skinner, while drinks expert Olly Smith selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: Born in 1962 to Nigerian parents studying medicine in London, Kemi Nevins now runs a successful cafe in Abercynon serving up delicious food. However, none of that she offers reflects her Nigerian background. Now Kemi's children are adults themselves, they would like to know more about their heritage, so the Welsh-Italian cook visits Kemi to help her reconnect to her roots through her love of food(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester City v Leicester City in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple looking to move to the rural setting of the Scottish Borders on a budget of £850,000, having lived in the city of Brussels for the last 20 years(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The team transforms items reclaimed from a skip in Walsall in the West Midlands, including a leather chair, metal shoe lasts and four kitchen chairs(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: England midfielder Jill Scott, Olympic Taekwondo silver medallist Lutalo Muhammad, rugby league great Kevin Sinfield and Great Britain 400m runner Emily Diamond join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the showpiece match (Kick-off 5.30pm), which comes from Khalifa International Stadium in Doha. Champions League title-holders Liverpool and Copa Libertadores winners Flamengo were the pre-tournament favourites to reach the final and compete for the trophy that has been won by Real Madrid in each of the last three years(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the high-tempo, music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a potential £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are loved-up couples Mark and Stewart from Leicester and Sindy and Vanel from London, along with uncle-and-niece duo Tony and Kelly from Belfast. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise hits from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Robbie Williams surprises some karaoke-singing members of the audience with special renditions of his classic hits in Robbie-oke, and gives a performance of Winter Wonderland. Plus, Ian Wright hands over his phone for Send To All, and three young brothers get the surprise of their lives when they take part in an Escape Room, but find themselves on stage at the London Palladium to become the Unexpected Stars of the Show(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby comes to terms with her pregnant sister's arrival, while David and Rosa celebrate a wedding. Charlie plans an early Christmas, but Duffy deflates his festive cheer when she tells him that she must prepare for Bill's visit and that they're going to go to the cinema together(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Leicester City at the Etihad Stadium and Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park. Last season's corresponding fixture between the Citizens and Foxes was a pivotal one in terms of the title race. Long-serving City captain Vincent Kompany's wonder-goal was the difference between the sides, and meant Pep Guardiola's men only needed one more victory to clinch the title, which they duly accomplished a few days later. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Southampton at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Burnley at the Vitality Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield United at the Amex Stadium, Newcastle United v Crystal Palace at St James' Park, and Norwich City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Carrow Road(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell present a look ahead to the penultimate round of the regular season, with fixtures including San Francisco 49ers v Los Angeles Rams(n)

programme.title: Looper (2012)
programme.desc: In the future, a crime syndicate uses time travel to dispose of people without trace, sending them back 30 years to be killed by hitmen. One of the assassins employed by the Mob finds his latest target is himself as an older man, and both versions go on the run as they try to change the course of history. Sci-fi thriller, with Joseph Gordon-Levitt, Bruce Willis and Emily Blunt(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Leicester City at the Etihad Stadium and Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park. Last season's corresponding fixture between the Citizens and Foxes was a pivotal one in terms of the title race. Long-serving City captain Vincent Kompany's wonder-goal was the difference between the sides, and meant Pep Guardiola's men only needed one more victory to clinch the title, which they duly accomplished a few days later. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Southampton at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Burnley at the Vitality Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield United at the Amex Stadium, Newcastle United v Crystal Palace at St James' Park, and Norwich City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Carrow Road(n)

programme.title: Britain's Christmas Story
programme.desc: Gareth Malone and Karen Gibson conclude their special series for Advent exploring Britain's Christmas traditions, unwrapping how the season has been celebrated across the centuries. They discover why a ban on Christmas led to rioting in the streets, and how the festivities were revived with a vengeance in Victorian times. As they trace the stories behind now-common traditions - and take part in less familiar ones such as pub carolling - the Archbishop of Canterbury explains how he celebrates Christmas, and why its meaning remains so relevant today(n)

programme.title: Home (2015)
programme.desc: An alien on the run from his own people arrives on Earth and embarks on an adventure with a daring young woman. It turns out to be a life-changing trip during which they begin to understand the true meaning of the word home. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin and Jennifer Lopez(n)

programme.title: Snow Bears
programme.desc: Documentary following polar bears as they travel 400 miles to reach the North Pole. As spring sets in, bears and their cubs must travel north to reach the pack ice, where they will hunt seals. Their journey leads to encounters with all manner of other Arctic wildlife, including reindeer, narwhals, walrus and arctic foxes. Narrated by Kate Winslet(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen looks through the archives to see how Wales has celebrated the festive season over the past 50 years. Featuring a grandmother who had her own festive grotto, a Christmas baby from 1971, a seasonal food hamper being sent to the Skerrie Islanders and a bus driver who keeps his passengers in the mood by wearing a tree on his head(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones celebrates Christmas with carols from the Assembly Hall in Belfast, including Hark The Herald, and O Little Town Of Bethlehem. With guests Katherine Jenkins, G4, Alexis Ffrench and The Lion King star Shaun Escoffery(n)

programme.title: Toy Story (1995)
programme.desc: A jealous string-pulled cowboy doll's status as his owner's favourite plaything is threatened by the arrival of a charismatic hi-tech astronaut figure, who refuses to believe he is simply a toy. However, the rivals are forced to work together when they are left stranded in the outside world. Pixar animated adventure, featuring the voices of Tom Hanks, Tim Allen, Don Rickles and Jim Varney(n)

programme.title: Penguins of Madagascar (2014)
programme.desc: Heroic flightless birds Skipper, Kowalski, Private and Rico uncover a plot to take over the world. Needing help to foil the evil scheme, they seek out a secret society of animal agents, led by a heroic wolf. Animated comedy spin-off, with the voices of Benedict Cumberbatch, John Malkovich and Tom McGrath(n)

programme.title: Merry Madagascar
programme.desc: The animals deliver Santa's presents after he crash-lands on the island, but they plan to use the good deed as an excuse to get back to New York City. Animated adventure, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith(n)

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and the team visit Tyntesfield in North Somerset - a stately pile that celebrates Christmas in high Victorian style. John meets the staff getting the place spruced up, and he gets measured up for his own period outfit. Anita Rani tries her hand at making eco Christmas cards, Steve Brown creates decorations based on Victorian originals, while Ellie Harrison discovers the estate's veteran trees and learns about the novel way they are protected from livestock(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: As the Magisterium closes in, Lyra learns more about Asriel's rebellion, but her assistance to him comes at great personal cost. The adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series reaches its conclusion. Starring Dafne Keen, Ruth Wilson, James McAvoy and Gary Lewis(n)

programme.title: A Christmas Carol
programme.desc: In 1843, miserly businessman Scrooge is visited by a ghost from the past on a freezing Christmas Eve and is forced to face the impact of his self-interested, penny-pinching behaviour on his own life and those around him. Peaky Blinders creator Steven Knight's adaptation of Dickens' classic, starring Guy Pearce, Andy Serkis and Stephen Graham(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action(n)

programme.title: Women's World Cup 2019 Review
programme.desc: A look back at the tournament from France, which saw USA enter as the holders and England look for a strong showing under Phil Neville(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser raises the stakes by bringing all four of his choirs together to put on a festive grand finale in front of a large audience. He visits the performance venue and realises the scale of the challenge he has set the choir. He's concerned that he needs to fill the venue to make it the best experience, and hopes that the choir won't be overwhelmed. The grand finale performance culminates with a surprise reveal of some more musical friends for the groups to meet and carry on singing with. But not before a rousing Christmas sing-song, tinsel and all(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Halifax looking around a terraced house, as well as casting his eye over a Wolverhampton semi. Martin Roberts visits Ashford, Kent to see a former pharmacy(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: There are serious concerns for a baby boy who arrives in A&E and the team members need to make a quick decision about whether they can help him, or whether he requires specialist care 225 miles away on mainland Scotland. Meanwhile, occupational therapist Marc Beswick returns to long-term patient Summer. The four-year-old has a condition that means it's hard for her to move her arms and legs. Marc has been working on her electric wheelchair, making modifications to make sure she can develop some independence(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a hammock, a two-in-one colander and dish and a microfibre glove towel. Presented by Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for Christmas bargains at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while presenter Charlie Ross heads off to nearby Goldsborough Hall to learn about a special Royal Christmas gift sent to troops in the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi realises he is facing a long stint in prison when his bail is revoked. Daniel gets to the bottom of Bear's ambilivance towards Jimmi(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: As the Kembleford Amateur Dramatics Society prepares to put on a pantomime, a grim incident from the group's past is unearthed. A body is found in the woods, and identified as a woman who went missing from a rehearsal seven years previously. An examination of the corpse provides evidence that a man who confessed to the murder was innocent, leaving Father Brown with a miscarriage of justice to put right in time for Christmas. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple in West Sussex to find their first shared home together on a budget of £700,000, and meets a craftsman keeping the art of trug making alive. programme.title: Test Drive
programme.desc: Grado hosts a car-based game show in which three teams tour Glasgow answering questions via a specially-adapted satnav that will determine their route to a well-known tourist attraction. Correct responses mean they will follow the shortest route, but if they are wrong there will be diversions, wrong turns and miles added to their trip(n)

Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin and Jennifer Lopez(n)

programme.title: Snow Bears
programme.desc: Documentary following polar bears as they travel 400 miles to reach the North Pole. As spring sets in, bears and their cubs must travel north to reach the pack ice, where they will hunt seals. Their journey leads to encounters with all manner of other Arctic wildlife, including reindeer, narwhals, walrus and arctic foxes. Narrated by Kate Winslet(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Edith and her family celebrate their last Christmas together before she and Phil set off for their dream retirement in the Mediterranean. However, things do not go to plan as Edith's son Roger decides to invite her scurrilous one-time flame Bob to join in with the festivities, and Edith's sister Joan makes it clear to all that she will not be watching the Queen's Speech anywhere else. Meanwhile, Queenie locks Sandra and Wendy into a wardrobe, and Phil finds himself talking to a ghost. Christmas special from 2018, guest starring Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones celebrates Christmas with carols from the Assembly Hall in Belfast, including Hark The Herald, and O Little Town Of Bethlehem. With guests Katherine Jenkins, G4, Alexis Ffrench and The Lion King star Shaun Escoffery(n)

programme.title: Toy Story (1995)
programme.desc: A jealous string-pulled cowboy doll's status as his owner's favourite plaything is threatened by the arrival of a charismatic hi-tech astronaut figure, who refuses to believe he is simply a toy. However, the rivals are forced to work together when they are left stranded in the outside world. Pixar animated adventure, featuring the voices of Tom Hanks, Tim Allen, Don Rickles and Jim Varney(n)

programme.title: Penguins of Madagascar (2014)
programme.desc: Heroic flightless birds Skipper, Kowalski, Private and Rico uncover a plot to take over the world. Needing help to foil the evil scheme, they seek out a secret society of animal agents, led by a heroic wolf. Animated comedy spin-off, with the voices of Benedict Cumberbatch, John Malkovich and Tom McGrath(n)

programme.title: Merry Madagascar
programme.desc: The animals deliver Santa's presents after he crash-lands on the island, but they plan to use the good deed as an excuse to get back to New York City. Animated adventure, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith(n)

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and the team visit Tyntesfield in North Somerset - a stately pile that celebrates Christmas in high Victorian style. John meets the staff getting the place spruced up, and he gets measured up for his own period outfit. Anita Rani tries her hand at making eco Christmas cards, Steve Brown creates decorations based on Victorian originals, while Ellie Harrison discovers the estate's veteran trees and learns about the novel way they are protected from livestock(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: As the Magisterium closes in, Lyra learns more about Asriel's rebellion, but her assistance to him comes at great personal cost. The adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series reaches its conclusion. Starring Dafne Keen, Ruth Wilson, James McAvoy and Gary Lewis(n)

programme.title: A Christmas Carol
programme.desc: In 1843, miserly businessman Scrooge is visited by a ghost from the past on a freezing Christmas Eve and is forced to face the impact of his self-interested, penny-pinching behaviour on his own life and those around him. Peaky Blinders creator Steven Knight's adaptation of Dickens' classic, starring Guy Pearce, Andy Serkis and Stephen Graham(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action(n)

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson is joined by Michael Stewart to present action from the weekend's Premiership fixtures, including Celtic v Aberdeen at Celtic Park and Kilmarnock v Motherwell at Rugby Park(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser raises the stakes by bringing all four of his choirs together to put on a festive grand finale in front of a large audience. He visits the performance venue and realises the scale of the challenge he has set the choir. He's concerned that he needs to fill the venue to make it the best experience, and hopes that the choir won't be overwhelmed. The grand finale performance culminates with a surprise reveal of some more musical friends for the groups to meet and carry on singing with. But not before a rousing Christmas sing-song, tinsel and all(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Halifax looking around a terraced house, as well as casting his eye over a Wolverhampton semi. Martin Roberts visits Ashford, Kent to see a former pharmacy(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: There are serious concerns for a baby boy who arrives in A&E and the team members need to make a quick decision about whether they can help him, or whether he requires specialist care 225 miles away on mainland Scotland. Meanwhile, occupational therapist Marc Beswick returns to long-term patient Summer. The four-year-old has a condition that means it's hard for her to move her arms and legs. Marc has been working on her electric wheelchair, making modifications to make sure she can develop some independence(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a hammock, a two-in-one colander and dish and a microfibre glove towel. Presented by Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for Christmas bargains at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while presenter Charlie Ross heads off to nearby Goldsborough Hall to learn about a special Royal Christmas gift sent to troops in the Second World War(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi realises he is facing a long stint in prison when his bail is revoked. Daniel gets to the bottom of Bear's ambilivance towards Jimmi(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: As the Kembleford Amateur Dramatics Society prepares to put on a pantomime, a grim incident from the group's past is unearthed. A body is found in the woods, and identified as a woman who went missing from a rehearsal seven years previously. An examination of the corpse provides evidence that a man who confessed to the murder was innocent, leaving Father Brown with a miscarriage of justice to put right in time for Christmas. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple in West Sussex to find their first shared home together on a budget of £700,000, and meets a craftsman keeping the art of trug making alive. The presenter also finds time to meet master craftsmen keeping the county's traditional trug-making heritage alive(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Andy and Danielle have moved into a new home in Teignmouth, Devon, and are desperate for a garden that reminds them of their wonderful holidays in Morocco. Luckily for them, Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich hope to make their dream a reality and design a Marrakesh rhiad-inspired back garden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Caner Rachael South restores a wicker cradle that has been in use for generations. With a new addition to the family on the way the owner hopes to be able to pass this on to her grandchild and keep the heirloom alive. Meanwhile, Brenton West fixes a much-loved powder compact, made in the shape of a piano. Toy restorers Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch add the finishing touch with a new, hand-sewn pink powder pouf and a wall clock is also rejuvenated(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: England midfielder Jill Scott, Olympic Taekwondo silver medallist Lutalo Muhammad, rugby league great Kevin Sinfield and Great Britain 400m runner Emily Diamond join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat tries to charm Kheerat in the hope of keeping her home - and while he agrees, he increases their rent at the same time, which does not go down well with Mo and Jean. Sharon and Louise assume that Phil's bad mood is down to baby Peggy still being in hospital. Jay leaves Stuart in charge once more only to regret when he and Rainie cover the place in Christmas decorations(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Charlie Brooker assumes the role of host, with journalist and broadcaster Emma Barnett and comedian Phil Wang joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Sue Johnston, Alice Levine and Bob Mortimer join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves(n)

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: A special seasonal edition from the Island Arts & Civic Centre, Lisburn. where comedian Lucy Porter joins regulars Neil Delamere, Jake O'Kane, Colin Murphy and Tim McGarry to poke fun at the people and events which have been making the news at home and across the planet over the past week(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Taking a place on Graham's festive sofa, from the new blockbuster Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker are John Boyega and Daisy Ridley, alongside Superman himself, Henry Cavill, who is appearing in new Netflix fantasy drama series The Witcher. Ruth Jones and Rob Brydon, reunited for the much-anticipated Gavin & Stacey Christmas special, also appear. Plus, Robbie Williams performs a song from his new album The Christmas Present, and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The Priest takes Fleabag for some quiet reflection, leading to an unexpected revelation. After a day spent looking back on painful memories, she turns to him for solace, only to find more trouble. Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, with Andrew Scott(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The two finalists take on their last challenge to win an investment from Lord Sugar, with contestants from the series returning to aid them in their endeavours. In an intensive three-day challenge, the finalists must create a new brand for their company, produce a digital billboard and make a television advert, before pitching to Lord Sugar and a room full of industry experts at London's City Hall. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is crowned the winner(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: Tom Allen meets this year's winner and runner-up, as well as Lord Sugar, to look back over the past 12 weeks of the business-based reality show. Featuring contributions from former contestants and special guests(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Tina Daheley present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jane Baxter and Nathan Outlaw, plus special guest Frank Skinner, while drinks expert Olly Smith selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Ruba, Nana, Tatiana and Marcus - workmates at a language school in Glasgow - compete against each other in a series of baking challenges(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester City v Leicester City in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in rural Devon with an ambitious couple looking for a large country home and as much acreage as their £1.25million budget will afford them(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The team transforms items reclaimed from a skip in Walsall in the West Midlands, including a leather chair, metal shoe lasts and four kitchen chairs(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the showpiece match (Kick-off 5.30pm), which comes from Khalifa International Stadium in Doha. Champions League title-holders Liverpool and Copa Libertadores winners Flamengo were the pre-tournament favourites to reach the final and compete for the trophy that has been won by Real Madrid in each of the last three years(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the high-tempo, music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a potential £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are loved-up couples Mark and Stewart from Leicester and Sindy and Vanel from London, along with uncle-and-niece duo Tony and Kelly from Belfast. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise hits from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Robbie Williams surprises some karaoke-singing members of the audience with special renditions of his classic hits in Robbie-oke, and gives a performance of Winter Wonderland. Plus, Ian Wright hands over his phone for Send To All, and three young brothers get the surprise of their lives when they take part in an Escape Room, but find themselves on stage at the London Palladium to become the Unexpected Stars of the Show(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby comes to terms with her pregnant sister's arrival, while David and Rosa celebrate a wedding. Charlie plans an early Christmas, but Duffy deflates his festive cheer when she tells him that she must prepare for Bill's visit and that they're going to go to the cinema together(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Leicester City at the Etihad Stadium and Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park. Last season's corresponding fixture between the Citizens and Foxes was a pivotal one in terms of the title race. Long-serving City captain Vincent Kompany's wonder-goal was the difference between the sides, and meant Pep Guardiola's men only needed one more victory to clinch the title, which they duly accomplished a few days later. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Southampton at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Burnley at the Vitality Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield United at the Amex Stadium, Newcastle United v Crystal Palace at St James' Park, and Norwich City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Carrow Road(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell present a look ahead to the penultimate round of the regular season, with fixtures including San Francisco 49ers v Los Angeles Rams(n)

programme.title: Looper (2012)
programme.desc: In the future, a crime syndicate uses time travel to dispose of people without trace, sending them back 30 years to be killed by hitmen. One of the assassins employed by the Mob finds his latest target is himself as an older man, and both versions go on the run as they try to change the course of history. Sci-fi thriller, with Joseph Gordon-Levitt, Bruce Willis and Emily Blunt(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Leicester City at the Etihad Stadium and Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park. programme.title: Britain's Christmas Story
programme.desc: Gareth Malone and Karen Gibson conclude their special series for Advent exploring Britain's Christmas traditions, unwrapping how the season has been celebrated across the centuries. They discover why a ban on Christmas led to rioting in the streets, and how the festivities were revived with a vengeance in Victorian times. As they trace the stories behind now-common traditions - and take part in less familiar ones such as pub carolling - the Archbishop of Canterbury explains how he celebrates Christmas, and why its meaning remains so relevant today(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is joined by antiques dealers Tony Geering, James Layte, Corrie Jeffrey and Alison Chapman in Scarborough, North Yorkshire, to help members of the public sort the treasures from their trash. Tony blows someone else's trumpet, James gets danced out of his dosh, Alison drops thousands on a claret jug, while Corrie gets her hand on a sparkling solitaire ring(n)

programme.title: The Switch
programme.desc: Host Sanjeev Bhaskar invites four new contestants to accumulate big cash totals by answering super quick-fire easy money questions. However, in order to take home that cash, they must survive The Switch, a series of three super hard multiple-choice questions that each has only one incorrect choice(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy opens up to Vanessa, Aaron looks for a distraction, and Sam gets an idea(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Robert's court hearing is delayed when new information comes to light, and Gary gets wind of Derek's plot to sell the factory. Meanwhile, Roy attempts to make peace with Nina(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street at Christmas
programme.desc: A look at the rich seam of Weatherfield's archive to showcase the stories that have entertained millions over the festive season for almost six decades - from weddings and babies to flings and fights, not forgetting those Christmas dinner-disasters and poignant goodbyes. Contributors including Barbara Knox, Chris Gascoyne, Jane Danson, William Roache, Sue Nicholls, Simon Gregson, Jack P Shepherd, Helen Flanagan, Thelma Barlow and Julie Goodyear share their memories of watching numerous, big Christmas storylines. Narrated by Sue Johnston(n)

programme.title: Ball & Boe: A Very Merry Christmas
programme.desc: After the huge success of their previous albums and sold-out tours, Michael Ball and Alfie Boe reunite for a festive night of music. Showcasing both their unique blend of extraordinary talent and infectious humour, the duo perform exciting new material alongside some Christmas favourites, as well as singing with special guests Shirley Bassey, Luke Evans and Gregory Porter(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh's Late Night Guest List
programme.desc: The host is joined by some of his celebrity friends who, as well as taking part in games, will help one contestant win their place on the best guest-lists in the world. In the first edition, Bradley is joined by This Morning presenter Holly Willoughby, Baby Spice Emma Bunton and her fiance Jade Jones, and actor Nigel Havers(n)

programme.title: Meet the Parents
programme.desc: Manchester's Olivia Davidson-Slack, and Will Oakley from Southampton take part in the dating show, hosted by Holly Willoughby. Both Olivia and Will must decide which of three potential suitors they would like to accompany on a date, but must make their choice by interviewing their would-be sweetheart's families(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The popular halfwit tries to be romantic, but his idea of what that means proves to be very different to Irma's. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: Monsters and ghouls get in the way of the Scooby gang's adventures as they travel and spend time together in the summer(n)

programme.title: The Sara Cox Show
programme.desc: Sara is joined by Emmerdale's Isabel Hodgins and Susan Cookson, actor Steve Pemberton and comedian Jayde Adams. There is also live music from Beverley Knight(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: The chef is back in the kitchen with festive food and guests including radio DJ Chris Evans, Gavin & Stacey star Joanna Page and Viscountess Emma Weymouth(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner reveal their take on classic festive dishes, including the perfect turkey, roast potatoes, and a classic Christmas cake(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha creates an American-themed meal with Christian Stevenson - aka DJ BBQ. On the menu are Maryland home fries for breakfast, buttermilk fried chicken for lunch, Christian's Smash Burger and tomato pie for dinner, with Nadia's Washington apple crisp for dessert(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. A phone number on the dead man's blotter sends the duo to the seaside in search of his latest targets - whose boat trip could conceal a motive for the killing. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the comedian meets a Pyrenean mountain dog that has a problem with its bladder. Elsewhere, a dog suffering from skin problems needs surgery on its eyes, and Paul also helps a West Highland white terrier that is almost twice the weight it should be get into shape(n)

programme.title: Shrek (2001)
programme.desc: Anti-social ogre Shrek resolves to stop an evil ruler from banishing fairy-tale characters to his swampland home. However, the reluctant hero ends up forced to rescue the beautiful Princess Fiona from a castle guarded by a dragon, aided only by a wisecracking donkey. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, John Lithgow, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy and Vincent Cassel(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a festive episode in which the Rev Kate Bottley, Dr Ranj Singh, Made in Chelsea's Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo and comedian Ed Byrne pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a festive special of the game show from Manchester, where he sets members of the public unusual Christmas-themed challenges and unwraps a variety of hilarious games(n)

programme.title: Casino Royale (2006)
programme.desc: James Bond achieves his '00' status and is dispatched on the trail of Le Chiffre, an international criminal financing terrorist organisations around the world. Bond's investigation leads to a casino in Montenegro, where he takes part in a high-stakes poker game in a bid to bankrupt Le Chiffre and put an end to his plans. Spy thriller, directed by Martin Campbell and starring Daniel Craig in his first outing as 007, with Eva Green, Mads Mikkelsen, Judi Dench and Giancarlo Giannini(n)

programme.title: Basic Instinct (1992)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking San Francisco cop investigates the case of a man murdered with an ice-pick, but his normally well-honed intuition starts letting him down as he falls for the sultry and manipulative chief suspect, the victim's former girlfriend. Thriller from director Paul Verhoeven, starring Sharon Stone, Michael Douglas, George Dzundza and Jeanne Tripplehorn(n)

programme.title: What Would Be Your Miracle?
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents the stories of people whose lives are being transformed by modern medicine, and follows them as they make their wildest dreams come true. In the opening episode, Emma meets Adele and Ashley from Aberdare, Wales, who are desperate to see their son Garin, who has cerebral palsy, walk for the first time. Now, thanks to surgery in America, there's every chance their dream could come true. And if it does, Garin has one further dream - to play football. Plus, the story of Lancashire woman Andrea, who has been deaf since childhood but is now also going blind. Will a cochlear implant allow her to hear for the first time in decades?(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: How to Spend It Well at Christmas with Phillip Schofield
programme.desc: The This Morning presenter returns to put more Christmas gifts to the test, aided by his wife Steph, celebrity guests and members of the public. They begin by gearing up for Black Friday with tips on the best gifts and gadgets on the market. With sales of personal electric vehicles on the up, Corrie trio Jack P Shepherd (David), Alan Halsall (Tyrone) and Colson Smith (Craig) put hoverboards and scooters to the test. Clare Balding is on a mission to find the perfect present for pets and Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards ranks gift experiences such as track racing, skydiving and flyboarding(n)

programme.title: The Sara Cox Show
programme.desc: Sara is joined by author Marian Keyes and sports pundit Chris Kamara, and there is live music from Will Young. Plus, some last-minute gift ideas for under £20 and a Christmas breakfast feast from Georgina Hayden(n)

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Juliet conjures up some ideas for Christmas, including baked party canapes, a brussels sprouts tarte tatin and a spectacular chocolate orange profiteroles tower. Liam Charles pays a visit to serve up Christmas lunch on a sheet of ruff puff pastry and Juliet visits chef Chantelle Nicholson in her London restaurant to sample a vegan main course Christmas meal(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John and Lisa cook up some family festive favourites, including glazed ham, a baked Tunworth cheese with onions and potatoes, and mini turkey and ham pies. Plus, Lisa decorates the tree with some beautiful Christmas biscuits(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha and Alex Hollywood help to prepare a festive family feast, including Nadia's annual tradition of hot sausage and cheddar rolls for breakfast, ginger gammon with tartiflette for lunch, a recipe for the ultimate turkey dinner, and Alex's delicious alternative to dessert with a white chocolate, raspberry and cranberry brioche for pudding(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead(n)

programme.title: How to Spend It Well at Christmas with Phillip Schofield
programme.desc: The presenter takes a look at the best Christmas deals on food and drink. The stars of The Real Housewives of Cheshire take a blind taste test to see if they can tell the difference between supermarket sparkling wine and real champagne. Mother and daughter Janet Ellis and Sophie Ellis-Bextor get to grips with some 1970s-style retro kitchen appliances, and Matt Tebbutt samples Phillip's efforts with a DIY home gin-making kit(n)

programme.title: The Big Quiz
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the annual clash between ITV's biggest soaps, with team captains Jack P Shepherd (David Platt) and Mark Charnock (Marlon Dingle) returning to lead colleagues from Coronation Street and Emmerdale in a battle to find out who knows their soap best. Lining up from the cobbles are Sue Nicholls (Audrey Roberts), Alan Halsall (Tyrone Dobbs) and Alexandra Mardell (Emma Brooker), while making their way from the Dales are Lisa Riley (Mandy Dingle), Isabel Hodgins (Victoria Sugden) and Nick Miles (Jimmy King)(n)

programme.title: The Pride of Britain Awards
programme.desc: Carol Vorderman, who is synonymous with the uplifting annual event, hits the road to visit some of the ceremony's winners over its two-decade history. In Torbay, she catches up with winners of the Emergency Services award - six of the seven crew of the RNLI who risked their lives to save eight stranded seamen. Carol walks the walk with fundraiser of the year 2008 Nina Barough and does a milk round with crime-fighting milkman and now MBE Tony Fowler. She also meets Rachel Edwards, who aged seven collected a Child of Courage award in 2001(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a Christmas Celebrity Special of the game show, with Davina McCall, Jarvis Cocker and Jo Brand hoping to win the £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity. That's if they can guess what Mr Chips is doing(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby present the launch show for the 12th series of the skating challenge. The famous faces taking part this year are magician Ben Hanlin, reality TV star Maura Higgins, Diversity member Perri Kiely, model and businesswoman Caprice, TV presenter Trisha Goddard, actress Lisa George, Paralympian Libby Clegg, ex-EastEnder Joe Swash, Steps singer Ian 'H' Watkins, newsreader Lucrezia Millarini, and former footballer Kevin Kilbane. Also appearing here is TV personality Michael Barrymore, although a broken wrist suffered after the recording of this programme has ruled him out of the main show, and he is replaced by former Blue Peter host Radzi Chinyanganya. In this first edition, the celebrities are paired up with their professional partners, while Torvill and Dean also perform a spectacular routine with some special guests, before taking to the judges' desk alongside Ashley Banjo and newcomer John Barrowman(n)

programme.title: Quantum of Solace (2008)
programme.desc: James Bond sets out on a personal mission of vengeance as he pursues the secret criminal organisation Quantum. His search leads him to the head of an ecological corporation, who is plotting to orchestrate a coup in a Latin American country to get his hands on a precious natural resource. Spy thriller sequel, starring Daniel Craig, Olga Kurylenko, Judi Dench, Gemma Arterton and Mathieu Amalric(n)

programme.title: Ball & Boe: A Very Merry Christmas
programme.desc: After the huge success of their previous albums and sold-out tours, Michael Ball and Alfie Boe reunite for a festive night of music. Showcasing both their unique blend of extraordinary talent and infectious humour, the duo perform exciting new material alongside some Christmas favourites, as well as singing with special guests Shirley Bassey, Luke Evans and Gregory Porter(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is joined by antiques dealers Tony Geering, James Layte, Corrie Jeffrey and Alison Chapman in Scarborough, North Yorkshire, to help members of the public sort the treasures from their trash. Tony blows someone else's trumpet, James gets danced out of his dosh, Alison drops thousands on a claret jug, while Corrie gets her hand on a sparkling solitaire ring(n)

programme.title: The Switch
programme.desc: Host Sanjeev Bhaskar invites four new contestants to accumulate big cash totals by answering super quick-fire easy money questions. However, in order to take home that cash, they must survive The Switch, a series of three super hard multiple-choice questions that each has only one incorrect choice(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy opens up to Vanessa, Aaron looks for a distraction, and Sam gets an idea(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Robert's court hearing is delayed when new information comes to light, and Gary gets wind of Derek's plot to sell the factory. Meanwhile, Roy attempts to make peace with Nina(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street at Christmas
programme.desc: A look at the rich seam of Weatherfield's archive to showcase the stories that have entertained millions over the festive season for almost six decades - from weddings and babies to flings and fights, not forgetting those Christmas dinner-disasters and poignant goodbyes. Contributors including Barbara Knox, Chris Gascoyne, Jane Danson, William Roache, Sue Nicholls, Simon Gregson, Jack P Shepherd, Helen Flanagan, Thelma Barlow and Julie Goodyear share their memories of watching numerous, big Christmas storylines. Narrated by Sue Johnston(n)

programme.title: Ball & Boe: A Very Merry Christmas
programme.desc: After the huge success of their previous albums and sold-out tours, Michael Ball and Alfie Boe reunite for a festive night of music. Showcasing both their unique blend of extraordinary talent and infectious humour, the duo perform exciting new material alongside some Christmas favourites, as well as singing with special guests Shirley Bassey, Luke Evans and Gregory Porter(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh's Late Night Guest List
programme.desc: The host is joined by some of his celebrity friends who, as well as taking part in games, will help one contestant win their place on the best guest-lists in the world. In the first edition, Bradley is joined by This Morning presenter Holly Willoughby, Baby Spice Emma Bunton and her fiance Jade Jones, and actor Nigel Havers(n)

programme.title: Meet the Parents
programme.desc: Manchester's Olivia Davidson-Slack, and Will Oakley from Southampton take part in the dating show, hosted by Holly Willoughby. Both Olivia and Will must decide which of three potential suitors they would like to accompany on a date, but must make their choice by interviewing their would-be sweetheart's families(n)

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: Monsters and ghouls get in the way of the Scooby gang's adventures as they travel and spend time together in the summer(n)

programme.title: The Sara Cox Show
programme.desc: Sara is joined by Emmerdale's Isabel Hodgins and Susan Cookson, actor Steve Pemberton and comedian Jayde Adams. There is also live music from Beverley Knight(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: The chef is back in the kitchen with festive food and guests including radio DJ Chris Evans, Gavin & Stacey star Joanna Page and Viscountess Emma Weymouth(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner reveal their take on classic festive dishes, including the perfect turkey, roast potatoes, and a classic Christmas cake(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha creates an American-themed meal with Christian Stevenson - aka DJ BBQ. On the menu are Maryland home fries for breakfast, buttermilk fried chicken for lunch, Christian's Smash Burger and tomato pie for dinner, with Nadia's Washington apple crisp for dessert(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. A phone number on the dead man's blotter sends the duo to the seaside in search of his latest targets - whose boat trip could conceal a motive for the killing. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the comedian meets a Pyrenean mountain dog that has a problem with its bladder. Elsewhere, a dog suffering from skin problems needs surgery on its eyes, and Paul also helps a West Highland white terrier that is almost twice the weight it should be get into shape(n)

programme.title: Shrek (2001)
programme.desc: Anti-social ogre Shrek resolves to stop an evil ruler from banishing fairy-tale characters to his swampland home. However, the reluctant hero ends up forced to rescue the beautiful Princess Fiona from a castle guarded by a dragon, aided only by a wisecracking donkey. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, John Lithgow, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy and Vincent Cassel(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a festive episode in which the Rev Kate Bottley, Dr Ranj Singh, Made in Chelsea's Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo and comedian Ed Byrne pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a festive special of the game show from Manchester, where he sets members of the public unusual Christmas-themed challenges and unwraps a variety of hilarious games(n)

programme.title: Casino Royale (2006)
programme.desc: James Bond achieves his '00' status and is dispatched on the trail of Le Chiffre, an international criminal financing terrorist organisations around the world. Bond's investigation leads to a casino in Montenegro, where he takes part in a high-stakes poker game in a bid to bankrupt Le Chiffre and put an end to his plans. Spy thriller, directed by Martin Campbell and starring Daniel Craig in his first outing as 007, with Eva Green, Mads Mikkelsen, Judi Dench and Giancarlo Giannini(n)

programme.title: Basic Instinct (1992)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking San Francisco cop investigates the case of a man murdered with an ice-pick, but his normally well-honed intuition starts letting him down as he falls for the sultry and manipulative chief suspect, the victim's former girlfriend. Thriller from director Paul Verhoeven, starring Sharon Stone, Michael Douglas, George Dzundza and Jeanne Tripplehorn(n)

programme.desc: As the Griffins prepare for the festive season by decorating their tree, Meg finds an ornament of the manger and Peter tells the story of Christmas - with a twist. programme.title: American Dad!

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve and Snot are humiliated when they attend a 'cool kids' party. Intending to get their own back, the pair decide to arranging their own bash for other unpopular teens, changing their destinies, and prompting a visit from their future selves. programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Rallo is forced to spend a weekend at a retirement home, where he meets a Jewish man and learns about Hanukkah. Meanwhile, Donna encourages Cleveland to stand up to his father. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Mike Henry, Sanaa Lathan and Kevin Michael Richardson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.desc: The McAllisters realise they have been put in their place. Seth recognises a piece of village history. Pollard takes a revived interest in art. Sarah comes to a momentous decision(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An accidental shooting at Ashfordly Hall prompts Bellamy to investigate reports accusing acting estate manager Richard Bentham of exploiting youngsters for child labour, but his enquiries reveal a far more complicated situation. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is called in when David Kenworthy, the son of a prominent local family, is found murdered on the beach at Whitley Bay. A witness spotted arcade owner Larry Crowe arguing with David on the night of the killing, and a search of the suspect's property uncovers a bloodied baseball bat. However, Larry has a cast-iron alibi and it's clear he wasn't the only person the victim met that night. A second death then leaves Vera facing mounting pressure to crack the case, and struggling to answer the question of why David was drawn to the seafront on the evening of his father's retirement. Brenda Blethyn stars in the murder mystery, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The body of a man killed by a single gunshot wound is discovered on the moors and a burnt-out truck containing the remains of a deer is found a mile away. Joe is convinced the case is a poaching war gone wrong, but for Vera, the truth is never that straightforward. Delving into the victim's story, she unearths a sad childhood in the country and a troubled life in the city, scraping by as a novelist. Following the recent death of his grandfather, he returned to Northumberland after a 15-year absence to sell his inheritance to a wealthy couple - but pulled out of the deal at the last minute. Could his death be linked to a dispute over land? Brenda Blethyn and David Leon star in the murder mystery(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A casino is robbed, presenting Frost with a fresh challenge to his deductive powers, and things go from bad to worse when a local copper is reported missing and a well-known armed thug is spotted in Denton. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Downtrodden detective Jack Frost investigates after an unidentified man's body is found on top of a pile of glass at a council recycling centre - and the subsequent autopsy reveals a few surprises. Another case is soon on the cards when an apparent suicide raises suspicions. On the personal front, Frost decides to get in shape and joins a gym, where he falls for the charms of his fitness trainer. Starring David Jason and Claire Cox(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Du Plessis is hailed as South Africa's answer to Crocodile Dundee as guests arrive at Leopard's Den to watch him in action. However, publicity is the last thing the family wants when pet cheetah Jana runs away from a stunt and has an accident. Meanwhile, a man claiming ownership of a lion demands Danny hand it over - or he will report Jana's injuries to the authorities. Deon Stewardson and Stephen Tompkinson star(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: The residents visit an auction to buy a giraffe after their own is killed by lions, but when Danny and Alice argue over a suitable replacement, he is led into the arms of Vanessa. Meanwhile, an approaching storm means the family has to release some of the animals for safety reasons - but when it hits, Charlotte and Danny come off worst. Stephen Tompkinson, Dawn Steele and Mary-Anne Barlow star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is forced to change his unhealthy diet after the drivers are obliged to take a medical test. Vintage Seventies comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth receives several anonymous letters outlining a killer's intention to murder a succession of people in alphabetical order, prompting him to try to unmask the culprit before the body-count mounts. Crime drama based on the Agatha Christie novel, starring David Suchet, with Pippa Guard, Donald Sumpter and Miranda Forbes(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At a Christmas family get-together, a cracker riddle states two people will die before midnight on Boxing Day, and just a few hours later family matriarch Lydia meets an untimely end - giving Barnaby the perfect reason to escape a boring Christmas with his in-laws. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by Haydn Gwynne and Margery Mason(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's a time of joy and happiness in Midsomer for once - Christmas is coming, and DCI Barnaby and his wife Sarah are soon to become parents. But the festive mood changes when a man is stabbed to death with an antique sword during a ghost-hunting party at a spooky manor house in Morton Shallows. Is blacksmith's daughter Rose Wilton seeking revenge from beyond the grave, or will Barnaby and sidekick Charlie Nelson find a more earthly motive behind the murder? Starring Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee, with Fiona Dolman, Les Dennis, Elizabeth Berrington, Mark Heap, Emily Joyce, James Murray and Hannah Tointon(n)

programme.title: Hattie
programme.desc: Ruth Jones stars as Carry On actress Hattie Jacques in this dramatisation of her secret affair with a younger man in the early 1960s, while still married to actor John Le Mesurier. A love triangle develops between the three and Hattie uses her charm and ingenuity to control escalating family tensions and threats of public exposure - but must ultimately make a decision that puts her at risk of being left on her own. Also starring Robert Bathurst, Aidan Turner and Jeany Spark(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. The next morning, Alice decides to pack her bags and leave, but a kiss from Danny might prompt a change of heart(n)

programme.desc: The Ropers take in a lodger, and Mildred is delighted when he begins doing all the household jobs that George will not tackle. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

Rural drama, guest starring Graham Turner and Geoffrey Hutchings, with John Duttine, Steven Blakeley and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse

John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Bugsy Malone (1976)
programme.desc: Alan Parker's slapstick musical comedy, featuring a cast composed entirely of children and following a war between rival gangsters in 1920s New York - fought with custard pies and cream-firing Tommy guns. When a slick mobster acquires devastating new weapons, his arch rival enlists the aid of a small-time chancer to steal them. Starring Scott Baio, Florrie Dugger and John Cassisi, with Jodie Foster in an early appearance(n)

programme.desc: Feature-length festive special. But tensions between the couple mount when the prince invites house guests to join the celebrations. Meanwhile, Skerrett inherits a large estate from an estranged uncle. programme.title: George and Mildred

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's hopes for a night of romance are dashed when Chrissy decides to supervise his girlfriend's visit. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The McAllisters realise they have been put in their place. Seth recognises a piece of village history. Pollard takes a revived interest in art. Sarah comes to a momentous decision(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is determined to assert his independence, while Zoe begins to feel overlooked. The Sugdens face the awful truth together(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
