programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies challenges his five fearful funny people to entertain him by completing more baffling challenges, which this week involve corneas, crackers and construction(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Outnumbered star Hugh Dennis, presenter Ben Fogle, newsreader Kate Silverton and Strictly Come Dancing judge Craig Revel Horwood are this week's guests on the comedy panel show. Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack guide the contestants as they try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Presented by Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Deadliest Crash: Disaster at Le Mans
programme.desc: Documentary examining what caused Pierre Levegh's Mercedes to crash into the crowd during the 1955 Le Mans 24-hour race, killing more than 80 people - an accident that remains the worst in the history of motor racing. Among the theories discussed are the use of untested technologies by the car's manufacturers, the addition of a lethal fuel additive and allegations of reckless driving by future Formula One world champion Mike Hawthorn, who won the race with Jaguar team partner Ivor Bueb. Narrated by Jack Deam(n)

programme.title: Slow Train Through Africa with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, the comedian crosses 1,000 miles of South Africa, reaching the most southerly passenger railway station on the continent. Heading to Johannesburg, he reveals how the city has benefited from the scale of gold-mining in the area. Later on, he takes to the skies in a light aircraft for a bird's-eye view of one of South Africa's largest ostrich farms, and catches one of the world's most luxurious trains for a seven-hour ride to Cape Town, before reaching the coastal settlement of Simon's Town(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The experts head to a field in south Oxfordshire where significant findings were made by an inquisitive PhD student half a century ago, including a mosaic and the stone walls of a former Roman building. Tony Robinson and the crew examine aerial pictures showing clear building lines in the ground, and speak to the current landowner, a farmer who continues to uncover bricks and tiles. They try to evaluate the building's purpose and how the Thames may have shifted the remains over the course of two millennia(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team search for the remains of a Norman castle in Dundrum, Co Down, one of Northern Ireland's most picturesque counties, exploring a territory established by renegade knight John de Courcy in the 12th century against the orders of King John. The castle was later rebuilt, and much of its replacement is still standing, but the experts are convinced that some of what remains dates from de Courcy's time - and discover that it may go back even further(n)

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: A secret weapons program in the heart of Austria that could have ended badly for the Allies - and may have indicated nuclear plans being concocted by Hitler(n)

programme.title: Viking Dead
programme.desc: A look at the discovery of various sites where Vikings had buried treasure, examining why they did it and where the loot came from originally(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Engineering breakthroughs that allow trains to go faster than ever, including technology that allows the navigation of the winding mountains of Spain at speed.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how fleas are able to jump great distances for their size and attempts to discover how fast a cheetah can run(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores animals that have developed tool-making skills to help them survive, including orangutans and crows(n)

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores how insects compensate for their small size by banding together in vast numbers. He reveals that the operations of many of these societies are so finely tuned they appear to work as a single entity. Footage shows how African termite mounds act as huge ventilation systems of such sophistication that humans are only now beginning to unravel the mechanics of the structure(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Impossible Railways

programme.title: Churchill's Secret Son
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of Tipperary-born businessman and Conservative cabinet minister Brendan Bracken, who became Winston Churchill's right-hand man during the Second World War by opposing efforts to appease Adolf Hitler. The film considers the possibility of a biological connection between the two men(n)

programme.title: The Good Nazi
programme.desc: The story of Major Karl Plagge, a high-ranking Nazi officer who risked his own life to save hundreds of people, in a story reminiscent of Schindler's List(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: An examination of how Leon Degrelle and an array of Finns, Estonians and fellow Belgians joined German volunteer brigades to hold off the Red Army on the Eastern front during the Second World War and enabled 56,000 men to escape(n)

programme.desc: A profile of Robert Cecil, a formidable political operator who devised and established an extraordinary web of spies and informers designed to ensure King James I's security(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Acclaimed chronicle of the Second World War, beginning with an account of Hitler's ascent to power at a time when the Germans were stricken by humiliating military defeat, runaway inflation and economic depression. The Nazi leader's promises of regained national pride and prosperity struck a chord with millions of ordinary citizens, and he was elected on a tidal wave of popular support. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The months following prime minister Neville Chamberlain's declaration of war against Germany, in which the only casualties on the home front were those who had to endure the blackouts. Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis' strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the documentary exploring Earth's natural history and the factors that have affected it. The first edition examines how the sun affects life on Earth through the seasons, following a polar bear preparing its cubs for a long journey to avoid becoming stranded on floating glaciers. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: A tour of the mightiest rivers in the world, from the deep waters of Siberia's Lake Baikal, home to the world's only freshwater seal, to the wetlands downstream of Earth's tallest waterfall patrolled by frenzied piranhas. There is also footage of the lightning-quick ambush of wildebeest by crocodiles in Africa and macaque monkeys swimming underwater to catch crabs in Indonesia. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the world's caves, including the Lechuguilla Cave in America, which contains chambers filled with 6m-long crystals. He observes creatures in Texas and Thailand that never see daylight, and inside Deer Cave in Borneo he finds five million bats whose droppings support an entire ecosystem(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The nature series looks at animals that live on some of the world's highest mountains. Cameras show gelada baboons screaming to frighten off predators in Ethiopia, demoiselle cranes struggling to migrate over the Himalayas, snow leopards hunting on the peaks of Pakistan and a wild giant panda nursing a baby in a hillside cave. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Trains That Changed the World

programme.title: Slow Train Through Africa with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, the comedian crosses 1,000 miles of South Africa, reaching the most southerly passenger railway station on the continent. Heading to Johannesburg, he reveals how the city has benefited from the scale of gold-mining in the area. Later on, he takes to the skies in a light aircraft for a bird's-eye view of one of South Africa's largest ostrich farms, and catches one of the world's most luxurious trains for a seven-hour ride to Cape Town, before reaching the coastal settlement of Simon's Town(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: An account of Hitler's decision to invade the Soviet Union, which produced an initial series of dazzling victories until the harsh winter of 1941 took hold, with fatal consequences for the ill-equipped German army. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough focuses on animals living in deserts. Cameras explore the dry Atacama of Chile, where guanacos survive by licking dew off cacti, and capture the rare sight of wild camels eating snow to stay hydrated on Mongolia's Gobi plains. In the rocky Namibian desert, elephants desperately search for food and lions wander the wilderness hunting oryx(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Camera crews follow the activities of wildlife in the Arctic and Antarctic, including an emperor penguin colony's nine-month migration across sea ice to its breeding ground. The team also takes aerial and underwater shots to capture humpback whales feeding and tracks a male polar bear as it swims 100km to find new ice from which to hunt seals(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The wildlife on the great plains of Africa, Asia, North America and the Arctic, which cover a quarter of the land on Earth. The distinctive Tibetan fox is captured on film for the first time, while a herd of caribou migrates 2,000 miles across the tundra in search of grass. Night vision equipment tracks a pride of 30 lions hunting elephants in the tropical African savannah near one of the region's few remaining waterholes. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: An insight into woodlands around the world, from the stunted conifers cloaked in snow and ice in the Arctic forests, to the broadleaf woods in North America and Europe. Also featured are the 100m-high giant redwoods of California, as well as Madagascar's unusual upside-down trees, which store water in their swollen trunks and harbour strange wildlife. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

When the chef arrives he finds himself turfing out the existing customers for their own safety due to a cockroach infestation on the premises(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie and Frank accidentally crash their car through the wall of Ray's house, causing a bitter family dispute - and forcing Debra into the role of peacemaker. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert believes Amy is the woman of his dreams and decides to tell her the truth about Joanne and Stefania, but she is so shocked by the confession that she refuses to take him back(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz struggles to tell Alice her hamster is dead, while Niles and Frasier return to their childhood home and discover what they believe to be evidence of a murder(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Cam Winston and Frasier decide to suspend hostilities for the sake of their parents, who have supposedly fallen for each other - though their late-blossoming romance could just be a clever subterfuge. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to the Chiltern Hills in Buckinghamshire to meet Adam and Mandy, who run a classic-car restoration business(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Donna and Martin, from Dagenham, east London, seek a holiday home for the whole family to enjoy on the stunning Costa Dorada in Spain. They have a budget of £65,000 and want a one-bedroom apartment with outside space close to the bustling town of Salou. Jasmine Harman lines up five properties for them to consider(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Christmas
programme.desc: Four talented makers race against the clock to create unique seasonal decorations. Plus, some big knits for Christmas and two sweet mini-makes. Kirstie Allsopp hosts(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty, Kate Quilton, Matt Tebbutt and Helen Lawal present tips on how to feed family and friend better, cheaper and faster at Christmas. They test three novel turkey-cooking methods against the clock - including deep-frying a whole bird - and share time-saving tips for preparing parsnips. They explore whether champagne is worth its hefty price tag, investigate whether digestif drinks really do help after a big meal and explore the psychology of why people cannot stop dipping into the chocolate box(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Thirty-three-year old Lucy comes into St George's having had a severe epileptic seizure that left her unconscious, and consultant Manny reflects on the damage a seizure of such magnitude might have done to her body. Two-year-old Sophie is treated after her brother squirted her in the face with a cleaning product, while Simon arrives in the urgent care centre with severe pain in his neck(n)

programme.title: The Undateables
programme.desc: Familiar faces from the dating show share their plans for the Christmas season, including rock singer Daniel, who is celebrating his first anniversary with fiancee Lily, and has prepared a special festive surprise. Elsewhere, Christine takes her boyfriend Ray on a Christmas trip to see his beloved Leeds United play. Less lucky in love are 21-year-old Fran from Milton Keynes and 45-year-old Richard from Surrey - but nevertheless, both are planning to return to the dating fray(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of the best moments from this year's Celebrity Gogglebox, featuring celebrities including Rylan Clark-Neal and mum Linda, Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Chris Eubank and Chris Eubank Jr, Oti, Motsi and Phemelo Mabuse, Martin and Roman Kemp, and Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock. Among the shows under review are Love Island, Naked Attraction, Killing Eve and First Dates, as well as the films Titanic and American Pie(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week. The programme captures their instant reactions and lively discussions from the comfort of their own homes(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: This festive episode from 1996 finds the Craggy Island clerics and six other priests lost in what seems to be the world's largest lingerie department, with only Ted's quick thinking preventing the Catholic Church being rocked by a major scandal. His bravery earns him the coveted Golden Cleric award, which is when his troubles really begin. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Frank Kelly(n)

programme.title: The World's Greatest Kids' Films
programme.desc: A countdown of some of the greatest family movies ever made, from enduring classics like Mary Poppins to modern hits including Finding Nemo. Featuring contributions from actors and film-makers. Steven Spielberg reveals the original inspiration for ET, while Christopher Lloyd shares his memories of being transformed into The Addams Family's Uncle Fester and Who Framed Roger Rabbit's villainous Judge Doom. The programme also features directors Robert Zemeckis, Ron Howard and David Yates discussing the secrets behind classic movie moments(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Expensive Toys
programme.desc: While the general public search out bargain toys on the high street and online, the UK's millionaires and billionaires use a network of bespoke, high-end suppliers of luxurious gifts and entertainment. This documentary explores the world of the craftspeople and inventors attempting to realise the dreams of Britain's wealthiest by creating luxurious gifts and entertainments, from crystal-encrusted rocking horses to £22,500 doll's houses - not to mention the 20mph electric car that's perfect for any parent with £30,000 to spare(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Christmas
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp oversees proceedings as four sugarcrafters take part in a cake-decorating competition, and there are tips on how to make a Yule log(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Christmas
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp oversees four talented makers as they create a unique seasonal present. There are also place cards made with plants, and supersize Christmas decorations(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy and Gwendoline want to hire their chateau out for weddings, but things are not going to plan, while Clive and Tanith hold an open day at their hotel to win over the locals(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy, Gwendoline and Michael locate their heating problem, but it means Billy faces the tricky task of repointing the chimney 20 metres up - despite being scared of heights(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy, Gwendoline and Michael finally hope to move into their chateau so they can host a hen party, although the kitchen's unfinished. Meanwhile, Dick and Angel try to keep costs down when shopping for baths(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy and Gwendoline need help tracking down the wild boars causing chaos around their chateau. Paul continues the task of converting his water tower into an office(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: At Billy and Gwendoline's chateau, Billy and his father try to level a patio so it can pass health and safety regulations. As Paul and Karen struggle to get the office conversion finished in time, a huge damaged pine tree needs to be cut down(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to the upmarket quarter of Hollywood to help the owner of failing health-food restaurant Sante La Brea. As well as struggling to control his disrespectful staff, the proprietor receives a surprise visit from the police at the height of dinner service(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. The head chef's attitude to quality control is driving customers away, and it is up to Gordon Ramsay to turn the business around(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a mother-and-son team who have gone through 20 chefs in two years at their struggling Italian restaurant in California. The adjoining bar is a triumph, but the oldest dining establishment in town needs an overhaul if the inexperienced owners are to make a profit(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants that he set out to save, including The Mixing Bowl, The Olde Stone Mill, Peter's and Campania. Have their owners pulled themselves out of their nightmares?(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

Nevertheless, he sets about rescuing them from disaster and turning around the failing business(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Loyle Carner and his mother Jean, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Chris Kamara and his sons Jack and Ben, Example and Professor Green, Olly Alexander and his mother Vicki, Rylan Clark-Neal and his mother Linda, Denise Van Outen and her boyfriend Eddie Boxshall, Oti Mabuse and her sisters Motsi and Phemelo, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, Martin and Roman Kemp, Laurence and Emilia Fox, Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock, Emily Atack and her mother Kate Robbins, and Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv critique TV airing in mid-July 2019(n)

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A 71-year-old beekeeper is looking for a man with a naughty side to his nature, while a 21-year-old is reluctant to venture from his house, which has dented his chances of romance. Other visitors to the restaurant include a charity shop manager who has his wedding all mapped out and a Welsh hairdresser recovering from a string of failed relationships, who spends the evening meeting a part-time model(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Thirty-three-year old Lucy comes into St George's having had a severe epileptic seizure that left her unconscious, and consultant Manny reflects on the damage a seizure of such magnitude might have done to her body. programme.title: The Cure
programme.desc: In 2007, Julie Bailey's elderly mum Bella was admitted to Stafford Hospital with a treatable hernia condition. Eight weeks later she was dead, having endured appalling levels of care and neglect. But this wasn't an isolated case. Julie was determined that no other patient should suffer in the same way and she launched a campaign called Cure the NHS. Drama based on a true story, starring Sian Brooke and Sue Johnston(n)

programme.title: My Grandparents' War
programme.desc: In the final episode, the actress Carey Mulligan discovers what happened to her grandfather who travelled thousands of miles to fight the Japanese in the final months of the Second World War. Her grandfather, Denzil Booth, lost his closest friends on board Britain's biggest warship, as it was the first to be attacked by a Japanese kamikaze. Later in the programme, Carey travels to Japan to understand the culture of the kamikaze from the other sid, and learns of the horrors her grandfather faced when he arrived in Tokyo(n)

programme.title: Scotland's Scenic Railways
programme.desc: Beneath vast mountains and across open moorlands, Scotland's railways connect coastal towns and rural villages through a Highland landscape voted the world's most beautiful railway. This programme meets the people who work on the railway and the steam enthusiasts who safeguard these iconic routes, offering a glimpse into a diverse community united by a passion for the nation's railways(n)

programme.title: Buy It Now for Christmas

They provide focus-group feedback, before representatives from three retailers - Amazon Launchpad, Lakeland and JML - decide whether or not to place an order(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Brent Council officers find a severely overcrowded house in north London, while Harrow Council try to help a family forced to sleep in a single room due to leaks coming from the roof. Their landlord is reluctant to complete the work and has instead served them with an eviction notice. Plus, the case of a Birmingham landlord who wants rid of his troublesome tenants, who have not paid rent for more than a year(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: In north London, Harrow council officers investigate an overcrowded property. An inspection uncovers many issues, including an unauthorised loft conversion that is a danger to the tenants who sleep there. A couple in Suffolk agree to rent a property to its former owner, who now refuses to leave, and a single mum tricks her way into a Milton Keynes property, confirming she has paid the deposit having done no such thing(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The story of landlords Pauline and Simon, who bought a property blind with tenants in situ - not realising the difficulties that would ensue. It turned out their tenants were causing havoc in the neighbourhood and had more than a hundred arrests between them for drugs and domestic violence - and if the landlords did not evict them, they themselves could be prosecuted. Plus, a Slough landlord forced to call in an eviction expert when his tenant cut off all communication(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Housing officers in Harrow receive a tip-off about an overcrowded property and when they arrive they find 10 people living in dire conditions and a house that needs extensive emergency work. Squatters in Tottenham invade an elderly woman's home while she is away, ransacking the property and refusing to leave. In Waltham Abbey a seemingly perfect tenant forges her way into a desperate landlord's home, forcing him to move in with his parents while he struggles to evict his unruly occupant(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben try to drill through the locks on a property to evict the residents, only to find to their surprise that the door has been modified with special reinforced metal. Elsewhere, Delroy and Brian try to reclaim money a garage owes to a customer and are shocked by the condition of the vehicle in question, and a woman who has acted as guarantor for her sister's tenancy does not realise the extent of her responsibilities(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement agents evict a large group of squatters barricaded into a disused nursery in London, and have to resort to smashing their way in as they refuse to leave. Meanwhile, agents in Bristol try to recover over £10,000 owed by a plumbing business to a supplier, and emotions run high when they attempt to evict a tenant who pleads her innocence in West London(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: An episode combining new and unforgettable past cases. The High Court Enforcement Agents head to Manchester to track down over £5,000-worth of debt, and seize an expensive car in the process. Later in south London, the agents deal with a violent debtor who threatens them with a concealed weapon, and in Liverpool, they are met with lies and aggression from a mechanic who owes nearly £3,000(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A prisoner requests Hawkes' presence at his execution, and makes a confession that shocks the forensic scientist. programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate the shooting of a Native American chief on the subway, and soon put two people in the frame - a company owner and an Irish hurling team captain who were engaged in a fierce land dispute with the victim. However, a member of the dead man's tribe is soon suspected of the murder. Starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY

Guest starring singer Katharine McPhee, with Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A car chase through Manhattan leaves one man dead and a teenager injured, leading Fontana and Green to discover the victim was being followed after massacring a hunting party. Starring Sam Waterston and Annie Parisse(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A celebrity chef is suspected of murdering his lover, a TV network executive who had antagonised him by deciding to cancel his cookery show. programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a clothes shop owner during an apparent robbery. programme.title: Columbo: Any Old Port in a Storm (1973)
programme.desc: A vineyard owner's financial security is jeopardised when his playboy younger brother threatens to sell the family winery. However, the crumpled cop sees through the ruse and in the process reveals he is something of a wine buff himself. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Donald Pleasence and Julie Harris(n)

programme.desc: Sam's wife Michelle resumes her CIA cover to investigate a hit on a Russian mob boss with possible links to Sidorov and the stolen nukes. LL Cool J and Chris O'Donnell star, with Aunjanue Ellis, Daniela Ruah and Barrett Foa(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. Kim Raver (24) and John Corbett (Sex and the City) guest star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Logan and Wheeler are forced to explain their actions after the stabbing of a fireman leads to a massive brawl and an official inquiry. When questions arise concerning the victim's sexual preference, the detectives link the crime to a similar murder 12 years earlier - but a secret the wife has been keeping could hold the key to solving the case. Drama, starring Chris Noth and Julianne Nicholson(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Boyd Hartwell, a hedge fund manager away in Hong Kong on business, is forced to watch helplessly as a video call home is interrupted by an intruder, who proceeds to rape and kidnap his wife. A ransom is issued, demaning that Boyd offer up 250,000 dollars for the safe return of his spouse, but when the person collecting the money turns out to be the missing wife herself, Benson and the team realise there might be more to this case than they initially suspected. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The detectives try to track down a serial rapist, but have to contend with neighbourhood watch members who become vigilantes. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A missing teenager leads the team to a man preying on the daughters he fathered through a sperm bank. Starring BD Wong, James Van Der Beek and Eric Close(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Detectives Briscoe and Green try to discover the identity of a masked youngster who opened fire on classmates, killing four and wounding several others. Starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach, with a guest appearance by Carey Lowell(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor

Surgeon Gabriel Weston travel to the US to witness the remarkable treatment for a hospital superbug that cures within hours(n)

programme.title: Eat to Live Forever with Giles Coren
programme.desc: The food critic takes up three extreme regimens in an effort to push the limits of life expectancy and meets people from around the world whose pursuit of longevity is an obsession that dominates every aspect of their lives. He undertakes the calorie restriction diet, before going on one that consists only of foods hunted, fished or gathered by mankind's Paleolithic ancestors and ends with another made up almost entirely of fruit. Throughout the process, Giles's health is monitored by his doctor, who assesses the impact these dietary habits are having on his body(n)

programme.title: Bread (1971)
programme.desc: A group of friends camps out on what turns out to be private property, and gets coerced into redecorating the owner's house in return for being allowed to stay. The squatters plan to stage a music festival without the landowner finding out - and shoot a porn movie to raise the money. Drama, starring Anthony Nigel and Peter Marinker(n)

programme.title: Sparks and Embers (2015)
programme.desc: A French woman living in London ends her relationship with her boyfriend when she decides to return to her own country. On the day she is due to leave, they meet again for one last goodbye. Despite knowing their break-up is for the best, both find themselves tempted to stay together - but neither feels brave enough to say it. Romantic comedy, starring Kris Marshall and Annelise Hesme(n)

programme.title: Secrets of a Windmill Girl (1966)
programme.desc: A dancer at a famous Soho burlesque club is killed in a car crash. The establishment receives a visit from the police, who talk to the other dancers to piece together the details of the woman's life and the circumstances that led to her death. Drama, starring Pauline Collins and Martin Jarvis(n)

programme.desc: The QI host tours the City of London, discovering the hidden mysteries of this rich and powerful square mile. Along the way, he visits the Bank of England's vaults, witnesses high drama at the London Metal Exchange as dealers buy and sell stocks, and experiences Dead Man's Walk at the Old Bailey, where many condemned criminals trod their final steps. Plus, as a recipient of the Freedom of the City of London, Stephen finds out just what privileges this gives him(n)

programme.title: The Stuarts in Exile
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dr Clare Jackson reveals how the Jacobite campaign took place on a European-wide stage, rather than purely Scottish, and inspired a nexus of international intrigue and covert plots. Jackson explores why so many law-abiding Scots and English were prepared to support the Catholic Stuarts and how the new Protestant Hanoverian regime hung by a thread, as George I and II struggled to impose their authority on frequently hostile and anti-European subjects(n)

programme.title: The Kid From Canada (1958)
programme.desc: A boy visits his family in the Scottish Highlands, where his boastfulness gets him into trouble during a horse riding trip. Family drama, with Christopher Braden and Bernard Braden(n)

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: Brendan takes the tourists for a tennis lesson in Nice, and dreams come true as the group plays with dolphins at a marine park. However, there are more waterworks at the vote, and someone could be waving goodbye to the coach(n)

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The tourists arrive in the French coastal city of Cannes, where they experience a seafront mime class. Meanwhile, Brendan Sheerin judges a ballroom dance lesson, and the group learns there is no such thing as a safe vote(n)

programme.title: Stephen Fry's Key to the City
programme.desc: The QI host tours the City of London, discovering the hidden mysteries of this rich and powerful square mile. Along the way, he visits the Bank of England's vaults, witnesses high drama at the London Metal Exchange as dealers buy and sell stocks, and experiences Dead Man's Walk at the Old Bailey, where many condemned criminals trod their final steps. Plus, as a recipient of the Freedom of the City of London, Stephen finds out just what privileges this gives him(n)

programme.title: The One That Got Away (1957)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War adventure about a German pilot who repeatedly escaped from British prison camps. Transferred to Montreal, he managed to break out and cross the border into America in a bid to rejoin his squadron. Starring Hardy Kruger, Michael Goodliffe, Colin Gordon and Terence Alexander(n)

programme.title: The Case of the Frightened Lady (1940)
programme.desc: A woman employed as a secretary to her aristocratic aunt has to contend with sinister servants plotting behind her back and a scheme by her employer to marry her off to her own cousin. The situation is complicated further when the family's doctor is accused of murder. Thriller, starring Marius Goring and Penelope Dudley-Ward(n)

programme.title: Love Birds
programme.desc: Short documentary about one of London's last working class pigeon racing communities(n)

programme.title: Inside the Royal Wedding
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on preparations for Prince William and Kate Middleton's wedding at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011. The programme includes details of the bride's dress and the couple's honeymoon plans, as well as interviews with friends, old acquaintances from school and former royal staff(n)

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events showcasing London's vibrant culture scene.(n)

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: With the rebellion reaching boiling point in Ireland, Elizabeth sends the dashing Earl of Essex to help quell the crisis. Glenda Jackson stars(n)

programme.title: Dream the Impossible (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about paraplegic motorcycle racer and paralympic skier Talan Skeels-Piggins, who has founded a charity to train paralysed people to ride adapted motorbikes(n)

programme.title: London Real
programme.desc: Inspirational speaker Brian Rose talks to Gavin Schmidt about the danger of Global Warming and how to control it(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

programme.title: Winter Wipeout
programme.desc: Richard Hammond presents this chilly version of Total Wipeout, which sees the contestants wrapping up warm to tackle a snow-covered course with winter-themed obstacles and seasonal challenges. They must manoeuvre themselves past Granny's House, watching out for the angry dog nipping at their ankles and a front door guaranteed to smack them in the face. They then cling onto the ski lift to avoid plunging into the icy waters, before riding the conveyor belt that throws them on to those old Wipeout favourites, the big red balls - or, as they have now been christened, the big red baubles. Amanda Byram laughs herself silly at the side of the course(n)
